frames: The agent is in a low, crouched posture, with its body nearly parallel to the ground and all limbs bent.
It begins to push up, extending its front limbs while its rear limbs remain bent and in contact with the ground.
The agent's body is now highly arched, with its front limbs reaching upwards, and it appears unstable.
It collapses forward, returning to a horizontal position, with its limbs bending to absorb the impact.
The agent lies flat on its stomach, with all limbs bent and resting on the checkered surface.
It slightly raises its front limbs, bending them at the joints, while the main body remains grounded.
The front limbs extend further upwards, creating a slight arch in the body, but it maintains ground contact.
The agent settles back into a prone position, with all limbs bent and resting on the ground, appearing still.
